title: BEST OVERAL WATCH I HAVE
text:
Hey, I am a fan of sports watches because it is part of our modern life and keeps us up to date with an array of useful information and health status that we all should really not go without. This watch is my favorite overall because of the features offered and the easy of use once you learn the features. It is not hard at all but it took me a maybe a week or so to know how to do it. I'll break it down into a couple of sections I think you might find useful to know about.

Screen:
The screen is well-designed screen with easy to understand menus and very bright colors to help you see the different functions and status of each one in particular. It’s really cool. The display of the BPM is large and colors indicate the levels of intensity of your work out. I think the colors of each menu are a great help to understand your activities and goals in a clear visual way.

Features:
Swap 2 times to the left for the weather information and you get the current temp f

title: Horrible
text: This watch is horrible. It’s not compatible with apple iPhone , no instructions, no video , nothing please don’t buy this

title: elegant-looking smartwatch, sms/call notifications, long battery, waterproof
text:
i wanted a new watch (my old one broke) primarily to know when someone calls, texts, or emails me, and also to keep track of my walking and workouts. this watch does all that, it's waterproof (i wash it after each workout), looks elegant (looks a lot like the apple watch), and costs under $25. plus the battery lasts a very long time, which is a huge plus. you can recharge it for 10 minutes while you're in the shower every few days, and it will never run out of battery. will last even longer if you turn off heart rate monitoring. excellent bright screen, with adjustable brightness. "find phone" feature is nice too (i've already had to use it twice). for these reasons, i'm giving this watch 5 stars, though it's far from perfect.

i have two main issues with this watch, though at this price-point i wasn't going to knock down a star for these issues:
1 - heart rate sensor is ok (fairly good actually) for establishing average and resting heartrate. however, it's not at all ac

title: Wrist band broke easily.
text: With very light usage, the wrist band broke a month after purchase. Rubber material is too thin and broke, causing the watch ro fall to the ground.  As I am just outside of the 30 day return window, I am buying a replacement wrist band.

title: Not for hardcore step counters
text: It’s good with everything except counting steps, it doesn’t recognize most of them and I can be walking slowly and it alerts me that I’ve been sitting to long.

title: practical and motivating
text: I love this fitness tracker. It motivates me to reach the goal. It helps me see how my sleep quality is. Unlike other fitness trackers that lose time, this one shows a continued tracking. Others I have found have missing times in the sleep pattern where the watch did not track. This is not the case with this fitness tracker. I like that it interchanges the intensity of your movement with different exercises. Battery life is about 4 days and it takes about 2-3 hours to charge. It will let you know when your phone reconnects to the watch. I would like to see that it would let you know when it disconnects.

title: Great inexpensive Smart Watch
text: Great Smart watch!!! Charge is awesome!!  Sleep monitor is not really accurate but its eye opening deep vs light sleep. Does not update on its own. Have to physically reopen app. I am a Flight Attendant I dont always have that option. But I can countdown my flight times which is life changing

title: Careful Not as advertised
text: Need a smart phone  to operate this watch.  Not revealed in Web ad.  Angry about the deceiption.

title: Easily More Than 5 Stars
text: I bought it because of price and it's capabilities! And it is certainly full of capabilities. With the fantastic service from this company, I've learned to love my sports watch and can highly recommend this over the numerous ones of owned. I gave it 5 stars and that was easy.

title: Just arrived and charged. Not turning on
text: I order this with prime so it arrived the next day. I was so excited to use this watch due to the astounding amounts of amazing reviews . Everything in the box for the watch was there the only issue is it didn’t turn on even after charging more than two hours. Did everything I could and still nothing.  Already returning it . Buyers beware

title: Comes in handy
text: An amazing tracker/ watch. It does so much. It tells time, it checks your heart rate and keeps up with your steps. Its not easily manipulated so you get a fair amount of steps and not a lie amount. It has for workouts too. It can call your phone if you loose it so your able to find it. This watch comes in handy.

title: Horrible.
text:
I like the size. I tried 4 others that were all to large in the face. This is perfect. Only problem is, I can’t go to the exercise to start counting my step

PS. Do not buy. It doesn’t stay connected.

title: another piece of junk
text:
I have tried 5 or 6 of these fitbit-like trackers. I wanted one that did all the simple things, like step counting while hands are in picket or resting on leg while biking, 24/7 heartrate without having to press a button to measure, and all stats on a face. This had it, until 2 months later, it is stuck on "fully charged 100%" and cannot get off that screen.
Piece of junk, like all other wanna be trackers. Do yourself a favor and stay away from this one and all others that look like eye candy but are the lowest quality junk you can get from you know where.

title: The price.
text: I love this smart watch because it does everything I need it to do. Thank you Ronley Kydd.

title: Not working
text: Can’t make adjustments can’t put it back to factory

title: I Love It. Way better than a FitBit.
text:
A couple of months ago my coworker was trying to talk my into getting a FitBit.i asked what it did. He said "I can check my heart beat, blood pressure, how much I've walked in a day. My calories burnt. It even checks my q sleep cycles!"
I replied, "It kinda looks like a thick watch. Does it tell time?"
He said "No, I have to look on my phone to 1 see all this stuff."
I didn't want one, I knew I walked about 7 miles a day at work, I saw this, it's cool 1.i ordered it, it connects up my phone so the time is always spot on. With the manual watches, they would lose about 30 seconds a day. But with this I can set a goal for my steps, it tells me when I've reached out. I can set alarms on my phone, which helps with my breaks at work. I can see my progress immediately, without looking at my phone. When it says in the description, "It lasts 10 days!" Maybe if you turn on everything, but for whey I need, it uses maybe 2% of battery a day. I'm at work most days, so I can select

title: meh
text: It's not very accurate but at least I can read the dial.

title: GREAT WATCH AND GREAT FEATURES.
text: GREAT PRODUCT AND QUALITY FOR A GREAT PRICE, I LIKE ALL FEATURES ON THE WATCH SPECIALLY THE HEART RATE FEATURE AND THE DIFFERENT FACE FEATURES AND GADGETS, I DIDN’T TRY OUT THE SLEEP TRACKING COULD NOT GET IT TO WORK. ( MADE VIDEO OF THE FEATURES COULD NOT GET IT TO LOAD )

title: Too complicated for an old person
text: I ended up reselling this. It may be great for techies but it was too complicated for the person it was meant for.
